    10. HOW TO CREATE MEDIA THAT'S A TROJAN HORSE FOR YOUR ARTISTIC VISION
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=86088856, kind=text -->

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1>
<strong>PART I: MEDIA VS. CONTENT</strong><br class="softbreak">
</h1><p>People who use Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube aren't making art.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>They're creating content.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>But how about ARTISTS who use these same platforms to promote their art? <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Aren't THEY creating content also?<br class="softbreak"></p><p>It depends.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>According to Merriam-Webster dictionary:</p><p>Content = material, such as writing, video, or music that appears on a website or other electronic platform<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Media = forms of expression used to communicate ideas or feelings to a group of people <br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">Notice the subtle difference between these two words?</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/NNahfxORBG3szdTJPx7i_contentVSmediaTHREAD3.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/NNahfxORBG3szdTJPx7i_contentVSmediaTHREAD3.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><p>The definition of 'content' emphasizes "material that appears on a website". It lives and dies online.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Whereas the definition of 'media' emphasizes "communication and expression". It's not limited to appearing online.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>The person "creating content" FOR Tiktok doesn't have the same intention as an artist "creating media" ON Tiktok.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Changing the words you use to describe what you do, changes the nature of what you create in the process.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>People' creating content' are making sludge for the platforms. The platforms keep making money in exchange for providing a place for you to satisfy their "validation and attention" addiction.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Artists' creating media' are using their chosen forms of expression to communicate to a group of people. They're releasing Trojan horses of ideas into the masses.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/uE66PTRo4xKG2Yk0QvX0_arnold_daniel.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/uE66PTRo4xKG2Yk0QvX0_arnold_daniel.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">
<a href="https://www.instagram.com/arnold_daniel/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@arnold_daniel</a> If your visuals are worth 1000 words, then you don't need 1000 words in the caption. But everyone else... Give us context.</h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Content creators' interactions are always transactional.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>But artists interactions should always be about connection. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>How do you make sure this comes through?</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/96ktJNIbSRKVVf0VbjWi_adrodragynous.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/96ktJNIbSRKVVf0VbjWi_adrodragynous.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Start by refusing to call what you make "content".<br class="softbreak"></p><p>From now on call what you share on social media (video, photographs, and audio)… MEDIA. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>MEDIA = trojan horses for your artistic message.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1>
<strong>PART II: HOW TO CREATE MEDIA FOR YOUR ONLINE PRESENCE</strong><br class="softbreak">
</h1><p>MEDIA is not the art itself. It's a representation of the art that you share online to connect with your people.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>MEDIA = a picture you took of your painting. Or a video of you making music. Or a JPG of your poem written out. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>MEDIA is how you bridge the gap between what you do (your art) and the audience you cherish.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>When MEDIA is well done, this bridge is just as thoughtful and creative as the art itself. It may even become art in it's own right.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>When poorly done, it's… content.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>How do you make sure you're creating media? (assuming the art itself already has a vision behind it, of course)</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h2>
<strong>1. TAKE TIME TO CREATE SOMETHING WORTH PEOPLE'S ATTENTION</strong><br class="softbreak">
</h2><p>Never post to post.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Consider what kind of media (something that contains your message) would be worth people's attention.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Then take the necessary time to make it.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Often it's coming up with the initial idea that takes hours, but the execution only takes 5 minutes. That's ok.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>As long as you work on making it undeniable.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Every post you share should be a microdose of your artistic vision. (<a href="https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/enroll/lectures/23551748" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">See World Building modules</a>)</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/5Zr0WUXRF2jcIiZmkO8U_ethelcain.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/5Zr0WUXRF2jcIiZmkO8U_ethelcain.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">
<a href="https://www.instagram.com/mothercain/?hl=en" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@mothercain</a> photo dumps are a branding tour-de-force</h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Even if you're doing a "photo dump". Even if you're posting a "thirst trap". Be subversive. Make it a Trojan horse for something way deeper… </p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/s33OS26Ryue85H5JzCcj_sighswoon.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/s33OS26Ryue85H5JzCcj_sighswoon.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">
<a href="https://www.instagram.com/sighswoon/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@sighswoon</a> reels us in with "thirst traps" and memes but always delivers food for the soul</h5><h2><br class="softbreak"></h2><h2>2. ALWAYS GIVE CONTEXT WHEN YOU SHARE ONLINE<br class="softbreak">
</h2><p>Sharing content without context shows your audience that you take their attention for granted. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>You're not Kylie Jenner.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Celebrities are so famous that everything they do automatically has context. Their audience knows so much about them that anything they put online has an embedded narrative.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You're not there yet.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You're a new artist hoping to find your fans. You need to take steps so your future ride or dies "see" you.</p><p>Don't be lazy. Don't post something they can easily scroll past. Post thiings to make a connection.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>No one wants to see random iPhone b-roll video of your "art thing". That's content. If you want to make it MEDIA, tell us a story. Give us context.</p><p>Make it easy for people to resonate with what you're communicating.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/ZLtGQ1NSbibJZ0RN2RoH_bradleycopelandstudioCOMP.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/ZLtGQ1NSbibJZ0RN2RoH_bradleycopelandstudioCOMP.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">@<a href="https://www.instagram.com/bradleycopelandstudio/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">bradleycopelandstudio</a> There is no confusion when you land on Bradley Copeland's page what she does, and what she stands for. She uses her online presence to create a world that supports her values, belief and art.</h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/CzqbRPzRPuV217HxiX51_bradleycopelandWORDS.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/CzqbRPzRPuV217HxiX51_bradleycopelandWORDS.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">@<a href="https://www.instagram.com/bradleycopelandstudio/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">bradleycopelandstudio</a> giving us context in a clear concise way.</h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>Artists send me links all the time without explanation… Like I'm supposed to click on it, get blown away, and praise them for being a genius.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>This shows a lack of awareness.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You art doesn't "automatically" matter to anyone. Not even your family. Not even your friends.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You have to make it matter by showing you much you care about it FIRST.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>You do that by giving us context.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/pimMy5fbTFaR6Yrg3YJk_thesundropgarden.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/pimMy5fbTFaR6Yrg3YJk_thesundropgarden.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/3RaxbFgWRuqPbnLi4dHp_thesundropgardenWORDS.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/3RaxbFgWRuqPbnLi4dHp_thesundropgardenWORDS.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center"><a href="https://www.instagram.com/thesundropgarden/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@thesundropgarden</a></h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>No one can guess that the person in the picture is a stranger you just met. You have to tell us.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>No one can guess how you felt while you were there. You have to tell us.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>No one can guess that "this moment was when everything changed". You have to tell us.</p><p>Give us the back story to transport us into the world you're building. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Do it with the caption, within the video, or however your creativity tells you to do it. But always... <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Tell us why it matters.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Make it impossible for us not to see your genius.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Make it impossible for us not to want to share it with others.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Put the art in a frame.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Kj0AppZSQkm0YDWcuWlj_fairytailcrossing.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Kj0AppZSQkm0YDWcuWlj_fairytailcrossing.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">@<a href="https://www.instagram.com/fairytailcrossing/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">fairytailcrossing</a> Just because you have a 9 to 5 job doesn't mean you can't advance your art. Aren't you creative? Find a way.<br class="softbreak">
</h5><h2>3. STFU<br class="softbreak">
</h2><p>Stop with your un-edited word diarrhea. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Run-on sentences isn't more "raw and in the moment". It's lazy rationalization for being sloppy.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Be respectful of your audience's attention.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Treat the first thing you write as the first draft it is. Edit it to make sure it communicates something powerful. Let your words sit for a few hours. Come back to it later so you can make sure it reads well.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Save the stream of consciousness for your poetry. For captions and explanations— keep it direct and truthful. </p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/2lKqcaPASGUO5qtGi0MZ_favelabaaby.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/2lKqcaPASGUO5qtGi0MZ_favelabaaby.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center"><a href="https://www.instagram.com/favelababy/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@favelababy</a></h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>How to best communicate: <br class="softbreak"></p><ol>
<li class="">- Use short sentences.</li>
<li class="">- Keep it conversational (don't go into bullshit "writer mode").</li>
<li class="">- Highlight emotions. People don't care about what you did… They care about how you FELT doing what you did.</li>
<li class="">- Leave them wanting more.</li>
</ol><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OyIjerxSh2HYamSrfCAp_haniakanye.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OyIjerxSh2HYamSrfCAp_haniakanye.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">
<a href="https://www.instagram.com/haniarani/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@haniarani</a> <a href="https://www.instagram.com/kanyewest/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@kanyewest</a><br class="softbreak">
</h5><h2><br class="softbreak"></h2><h2>4. INVITE YOUR PEOPLE TO TAKE ACTION OFTEN.<br class="softbreak">
</h2><p>Being online is taking time away from you making art.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Being online is interfering with your ability to enter flow.</p><p>So if you HAVE to be online, it had better be for a good reason.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OowttESQl2PpKAHHU45I_fariha_roisin.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/OowttESQl2PpKAHHU45I_fariha_roisin.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center"><a href="https://www.instagram.com/fariha_roisin/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@fariha_roisin</a></h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>GOOD REASON = Helping you elevate your artistic career.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>GOOD REASON = Interacting with your audience.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Like asking them who you should collaborate with next. <br class="softbreak"></p><p>Like polling them to find out where their head is at.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Like sending them to sign up to your newsletter.</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/VeMVK50RjilUfHYmZTrT_truealevtina.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/VeMVK50RjilUfHYmZTrT_truealevtina.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">@<a href="https://www.instagram.com/truealevtina/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">truealevtina</a> is a Ukrainian anti-war artist-activist. While you overthink about if you're "good enough"... Other artists out there are burning with so much fire, they expressing the raw emotion of what they believe in. </h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><p>People are scrolling past 500 videos and photos a day. Much of it is baseless, toxic, content.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>So when you create media for these platforms, make sure you create work that reflects that you understand this.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Artists are competing against the lowest common denominator "content creators" who already know all this stuff.<br class="softbreak"></p><p>So you're gonna have to work twice as hard to create compelling media for the ages. </p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/onXY54GBS6OzsqnF4vV0_earlyrupi.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/onXY54GBS6OzsqnF4vV0_earlyrupi.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"><br class="softbreak"></p><h5 class="ql-align-center">Early <a href="https://www.instagram.com/rupikaur_/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@rupikaur</a> changing the game. Making social media work for HER.</h5><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h2>5. CREATE YOUR OWN VISUAL LANGUAGE</h2><p>Did you notice something that ALL the above examples have?</p><p>All these artists have created their own visual language with their media.</p><p>You instantly recognize their posts among all the copycat stuff online. They have a very particular way of expressing themselves online that sets them apart.</p><p>So while you may be tempted to use those same Tiktok fonts, and caption styles as everyone else-- It will date everything you do.</p><p>Content.</p><p>Question every choice you make artistically even though this isn't even the art-- It's just to promote the art. But put the same care into it and your promotion itself will become artful.</p><p>Does this sound like too much work?<br class="softbreak"></p><p>Well, that "work" is the difference between hearing crickets when you post-- or curating a successful career for yourself. <br class="softbreak"></p><p></p>
